channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Tiger's son comes up aces in PNC Championship with a hole-in-one
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods isn't the only member of his family to can produce big moments. His 15-year-old son Charlie made his first ace Sunday in the PNC Championship. Woods was more excited than if he had made the hole-in-one himself.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lindsey Vonn to resume her comeback in St. Anton in January after St. Moritz race canceled
item.description: Lindsey Vonn lost a chance to improve her ranking when the second race of her World Cup skiing comeback at age 40 was canceled due to strong winds and poor visibility.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dampf, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Steve Kerr sent a message to Warriors in highlighting one key play vs. Timberwolves
item.description: Even after a win over Minnesota, Steve Kerr had pointed remarks for his team about their style of play around Steph Curry.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: John Seidler takes over as control person of Padres after Peter's death
item.description: John Seidler, an entrepreneur and business executive, has been named the next control person of the San Diego Padres, pending approval by Major League Baseball, after the death of his brother Peter Seidler, the team's former owner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Caitlin Clark effect hasn't reversed the decades-long decline in girls basketball participation
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Plunging participation in girls basketball led one of Nebraska's largest high schools to cancel its varsity team for the year.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steph Curry, Warriors defense knock off Timberwolves on the road
item.description: The Warriors held Minnesota to 37 points in the first half and Steph Curry finished them out in the fourth.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Ravens take down Steelers to keep AFC North race open
item.description: The Baltimore Ravens punched their ticket to the postseason and kept their hopes for a division title alive with a win over the Pittsburgh Steelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas holds off Clemson's comeback to advance in College Football Playoff
item.description: The Texas Longhorns took care of business against the Clemson Tigers Saturday to advance to the quarterfinals of the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former walk-on DeLuca provides early spark, Penn State drills SMU 38-10 in CFP; Boise State up next
item.description: STATE COLLEGE, Pa. (AP) — In a time of incessant change, Penn State linebacker Dominic DeLuca is a throwback. The former walk-on's team might be one too. DeLuca was guaranteed nothing when he arrived on campus nearly four years ago without the promise of a scholarship or much of anything else.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patrick Mahomes plays through ankle sprain to lead Chiefs to victory vs. Texans
item.description: Patrick Mahomes battled through an ankle sprain to deliver a 27-19 win over the Houston Texans and put the Chiefs one win away from a first-round bye.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bronny James discusses the challenge of dealing with his mental health following cardiac scare
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — The final buzzer sounded Saturday, the South Bay Lakers had won a game and Bronny James seemed completely at ease. Big smile. Handshakes with everyone. Even did a little dance step as he got to the bench.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Women's prayer group alleges harassment from pro-trans activists during 'Save Girls Sports' protests
item.description: A women's prayer group that attended the protests wearing "Save Girls Sports" shirts alleged the pro-transgender activists harassed them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, the league confirmed. The all-time MLB leader in stolen bases and one of the games greatest leadoff hitters died in Oakland on Friday after a bout with pneumonia, TMZ reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Falcons expected to release Kirk Cousins after just one season following lackluster play: report
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons are expected to release quarterback Kirk Cousins after just one season due to lackluster play, with rookie Michael Penix Jr. taking his place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter Classic Day 3: Fruita bounces back, GJ Girls win thriller
item.description: Another loaded, action-packed day of hoops at the 2024 Family Health West Winter Classic is in the books.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Christian Walker agrees to $60 million, 3-year contract with Astros, AP source says
item.description: Gold Glove first baseman Christian Walker and the Houston Astros agreed Friday to a $60 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press. The person spoke on condition of anonymity because the agreement was subject to a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World Series champion Dodgers top MLB luxury tax at $103 million as record 9 teams owe penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers topped a record nine teams owing Major League Baseball's luxury tax this year with an unprecedented $103 million penalty, and the $97.1 million bill for the New York Mets raises their tax total under high-spending owner Steve Cohen to nearly $229 million.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA players ejected after squaring up to fight
item.description: Despite neither of them throwing a punch, Jordan Clarkson and Ron Holland II were ejected Thursday night after squaring up on one another.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz show some fight in 126-119 win over Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (ABC4 Sports) - Collin Sexton scored 30 points, Lauri Markkanen had 27 points and 14 rebounds and the Utah Jazz beat the Detroit Pistons 126-119 on Thursday night for for their second victory in 10 games. Keyonte George added 28 points for Utah.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kucherov has 2 assists as Lightning beat Blues 3-1
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a pair of assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 3-1 victory against the St. Louis Blues. Anthony Cirelli, Gage Goncalves and Nick Perbix scored for the Lightning, who have won six of their last seven. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 31 saves in the win.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cody Bellinger experienced World Series title with dad Clay at old Yankee Stadium
item.description: Bellinger won the 2019 NL MVP award with the Los Angeles Dodgers after hitting .305 with 47 homers, 115 RBIs and a 1.035 OPS.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ducks to honor fellow O.C. team at promotional night
item.description: If you're a fan of Orange County sports, you're in luck this January. On Jan. 7, the Anaheim Ducks are hosting Angels Night, an event that includes a baseball jersey giveaway for the first 5,000 fans in attendance. The game against the Calgary Flames begins at 7 p.m. Tickets begin at less than $30.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Boys Basketball: Santa Fe Christian 63, Coronado 39
item.description: Boys basketball action taking place at Coronado High School as the Islanders take on #4 ranked Santa Fe Christian.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: A parade, 5K and tailgate party: San Diego ramps up for Holiday Bowl
item.description: With the 45th annual DIRECTV Holiday Bowl matchup between Syracuse and Washington State just about a week away, San Diego is hosting a lineup of events leading up to the big game.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Boston's Sports Bar & Restaurant's 'Gather Your Herd' Sweepstakes
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — With the NFL playoffs quickly approaching, Boston’s Pizza is giving six lucky fans a chance to celebrate Denver’s postseason push at the ultimate football watch party. “We’ve got some really nice TVs in our bar,” said Brandon Cramer, Boston’s Pizza general manager.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5
